steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content: The key signal is the phrase “without betraying the vanity of the writer.” Gibbon expresses concern that describing his work's success will inevitably reveal his own vanity—something private he'd rather not expose.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
”Exposing” captures the meaning here: revealing or uncovering something private.

This directly fits Gibbon's concern that in describing his work's success, he cannot help but reveal his vanity.

The other three choices describe different concepts—abandonment, misrepresentation, and exploitation—none of which convey revealing something hidden.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
